evals.prompt: A user filed: 'Checkout is broken, order total is wrong sometimes.' That's the whole report. Before I write any code, what do I need to extract or ask for to make this reproducible?
expected_patterns: exact steps OR steps to reproduce
build OR version OR commit OR git SHA
environment OR browser OR OS OR device
input data OR cart contents OR account OR fixture
expected.*actual OR expected versus actual
frequency OR how often OR every time OR intermittent
timestamp OR locale OR timezone OR currency
anti_patterns: looks like a rounding bug, let me patch

evals.prompt: Walk me through the loop you use to go from a confirmed-but-messy reproduction to a clean one I can hand to a developer. The original repro takes 14 manual UI steps across 3 pages.
expected_patterns: reproduce OR confirm
minimize OR reduce OR shrink OR strip
isolate OR narrow
capture OR record OR evidence
one variable at a time OR remove one step
smallest OR minimal
still reproduces OR still fails
anti_patterns: skip verifying it still fails after each cut

evals.prompt: The bug exists on HEAD but the feature worked in last month's release. I have a deterministic command that exits non-zero when the bug is present. Show me how to automatically find the commit that introduced it.
expected_patterns: git bisect start
git bisect bad OR git bisect new
git bisect good OR git bisect old
git bisect run
exit.*0 OR exit code 0
exit.*125 OR skip
git bisect reset
anti_patterns: git bisect run npm test:all OR run the full suite
git revert to find the commit

evals.prompt: My bisect script keeps marking commits as bad for the wrong reason — some old commits won't even build, and the test runner sometimes fails on a flaky network call instead of the actual bug. Fix my bisect approach.
expected_patterns: exit 125 OR skip OR git bisect skip
build fail.*125 OR untestable.*125 OR cannot build.*skip
wrapper script OR wrap the test
distinguish OR separate.*build failure
deterministic OR stub.*network OR mock.*network OR offline
127 OR 1-127 OR range
anti_patterns: let flaky failures mark commits bad
rerun the whole suite per commit

evals.prompt: The bug only appears around midnight and depends on a random discount code and a third-party pricing API. Make the reproduction deterministic so it fails the same way every single run. This is a Vitest project.
expected_patterns: vi.useFakeTimers OR fake timers
vi.setSystemTime OR setSystemTime
seed OR seeded OR faker.seed OR fixed seed
msw OR setupServer OR mock service worker OR http.get
HttpResponse OR stub.*response OR mock.*response
frozen OR fixed.*time OR deterministic
anti_patterns: let it hit the real pricing API
sleep OR setTimeout to handle the timing

evals.prompt: Same midnight/random/third-party-API bug, but it's a browser flow we reproduce in Playwright, not a unit. Make that end-to-end repro deterministic.
expected_patterns: page.clock OR clock.install OR clock.setFixedTime
page.route OR route.fulfill OR intercept
fixed.*time OR frozen.*clock OR pin.*time
stub OR mock OR fulfill.*pricing
deterministic OR same.*every run
anti_patterns: rely on the real system clock
increase the timeout to make it pass
page.clock is not a thing OR use Date override only

evals.prompt: I have a clean deterministic reproduction of the bug. The dev hasn't fixed it yet. Write the regression test now and tell me what state it should be in before and after the fix.
expected_patterns: fail.*before.*fix OR red OR failing.*first
before the fix OR pre-fix
assert.*expected OR expect.*toBe OR expect.*toEqual
pass.*after.*fix OR green OR flips to green
confirm.*fix OR re-run after fix
commit the test OR test guards the fix
anti_patterns: write the test after the fix
skip.* OR test.skip OR it.skip the failing test
mark it pending until the fix lands
fix first then add a test

evals.prompt: The dev pushed a fix and says it's done. The regression test I wrote earlier now passes. Is that enough to close the ticket, or what else do I verify?
expected_patterns: revert.*fix OR without the fix OR temporarily remove
confirm.*still fails without OR test fails when fix removed
test actually exercises OR test catches the bug
passes.*because of the fix OR not passing for another reason
re-run OR run.*deterministic OR multiple runs
anti_patterns: no need to verify the test fails without the fix
passing test always proves the fix

evals.prompt: I've spent two hours and the bug from the report does not reproduce for me — but it clearly happens for the user. How do I decide whether this is a flaky test, an environment-specific bug, or genuinely not reproducible, and what evidence separates them?
expected_patterns: --repeat-each OR rerun.*same environment OR repeat the run
flaky.*same code.*same env OR passes and fails.*same commit
environment-specific OR env-specific
timezone OR locale OR viewport OR OS OR browser version OR CI vs local
match.*user environment OR reproduce their env OR replicate their config
data-dependent OR account OR specific input OR their data
anti_patterns: it doesn't repro on my machine so it isn't real
no need to match the user's environment

evals.prompt: Reproduction is done and the regression test is committed. Write the repro evidence back into the bug ticket. What goes in, in what structure?
expected_patterns: minimal.*steps OR minimal reproduction
environment OR build OR commit OR version
expected.*actual OR expected versus actual
introducing commit OR bisect.*commit OR offending commit OR root cause commit
regression test OR link.*test OR test file OR test path
evidence OR logs OR screenshot OR trace OR artifact
determinism notes OR seed OR frozen time OR stubbed
anti_patterns: no link to the regression test
leave the original vague report unchanged
